codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failure context, worker transcript skeleton, feature commit, and current finding-engine implementation/tests. In the current repo state, the dedupe/relocation logic still satisfies same-HEAD dedupe, new-HEAD last-seen updates, semantic relocation reuse, supersede-on-relocation-failure, and same-run duplicate collapse, with targeted DB-backed regression coverage in internal/llm/provider_test.go.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The go-backend skill still lacks guidance that DB-backed package-local tests are acceptable when a milestone references end-to-end coverage that has not been scaffolded yet.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff for worker session dd350468-f408-45f0-95bd-69e04d65a2c3 records skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false and explicitly suggests documenting DB-backed tests as the fallback when the planned e2e package does not exist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state still does not record that finding-engine rerun/relocation validation currently lives in internal/llm DB-backed tests because internal/e2e has not been added yet.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission features.json still lists go test ./internal/e2e/... for this feature, the handoff records that ./internal/e2e was absent, and .factory/library/architecture.md discusses finding lifecycle scope gaps but does not note the current package-level validation location.

summary: Pass. After reviewing the current code and tests, I found no blocking or non-blocking feature-scope defects in finding dedupe and relocation behavior.
